items.label: Books — AI writing / publishing impact
items.excerpt: Tim Ferriss reports his book catalog is on pace to sell 80% fewer print copies in 2026 than in 2022, with nearly all the decline since LLMs like ChatGPT and Claude became widely used — framing AI chatbots as a direct replacement for how-to nonfiction and signaling a structural shift for authors and publishers.

items.label: Books — AI self-publishing / indie author tools
items.excerpt: Mark Leslie Lefebvre (Director of Business Development at Draft2Digital) discusses licensing his own AI voice through ElevenLabs for passive royalty income, the value of in-person author experiences as AI floods digital content, and how indie authors are navigating a market where AI-mass-produced ebooks are reshaping reader expectations.

items.label: Books — AI reading assistant / ebook tools
items.excerpt: Amazon's new Kindle AI features — 'Story So Far' (spoiler-free chapter catch-up) and 'Ask This Book' (inline passage queries) — are rolling out to Kindle devices and the iOS app, alongside Kindle Scribe's AI-powered notebook search and insights, marking the Kindle platform's first major reader-facing generative AI integration.

items.label: Books — AI publishing industry
items.excerpt: The 40th Beijing International Book Fair (June 17–21) featured a new Internet Publishing Joint Pavilion showcasing AI-generated content tools and publishing workflow applications from NetEase, Tencent, ByteDance's Fanqie Novel, and 20 other exhibitors, signaling that AI-driven digital publishing is becoming mainstream infrastructure in China's book industry.

items.label: Books — AI in scholarly / academic publishing
items.excerpt: A Cambridge workshop report calls for shared infrastructure to make AI use of scholarly content traceable, measurable, and trustworthy — arguing that as generative AI systems are adopted across research workflows, the publishing industry must preserve verifiability and provenance in the scholarly record.

items.label: Books — AI reading assistant / ebook tools
items.excerpt: Google Play Books launches 'Book Insights,' a Gemini-powered in-book AI companion that lets readers ask questions about plot and characters, catch up on previous chapters, and clarify text — raising debate about whether AI chatbots embedded in ebooks enhance or undermine the immersive reading experience.

items.label: Books — AI writing / publishing impact
items.excerpt: Jane Friedman's curated weekly roundup covers Wiley's $49M in AI licensing revenue, the Dutch Bookpact.ai consent-based licensing platform, authors suing Anthropic post-settlement, and HarperCollins partnering with Toonstar to adapt books into AI-generated short-form videos — a comprehensive snapshot of the AI×books rights and workflow landscape in late June 2026.
